senses.definition: To scratch something again.
examples: The cat came back to rescratch the same spot on the sofa.
If the paint looks uneven, you may need to rescratch the surface before repainting.

senses.definition: To test or check a person or thing again, especially for problems or risks.
examples: All high‑risk patients will be rescreened after six months.
The company decided to rescreen its employees for security clearance.

senses.definition: To show a film or television program again.
examples: The festival plans to rescreen the classic movie next month.
Due to popular demand, the channel will rescreen the documentary this weekend.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of rescreen: tested, checked, or shown again.
examples: All applicants were rescreened after the policy change.
The film was rescreened at several international festivals.

senses.definition: The act of testing or checking a person or thing again.
examples: Regular rescreening is recommended for high‑risk groups.
The rescreening of staff revealed several errors in the original checks.

senses.definition: The act of showing a film or television program again.
examples: The rescreening of the film attracted a new audience.
There will be a special rescreening of the documentary next week.

senses.definition: Third person singular of rescreen: tests, checks, or shows again.
examples: The clinic rescreens all patients every two years.
The channel rescreens popular series during the holidays.

senses.definition: To write something again, especially to correct or change it.
examples: The editor asked the author to rescribe the confusing paragraph.
He had to rescribe the list because the first version was lost.

senses.definition: An official written answer or order, especially from someone in authority.
examples: The emperor issued a rescript to clarify the law.
The rescript was read aloud to the assembled council.

senses.definition: To write something again or in a different form.
examples: The speech was rescripted to suit the new audience.
The playwright decided to rescript the final scene.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of rescript: written again or in a different form.
examples: The dialogue was completely rescripted after the first rehearsal.
Several scenes were rescripted to shorten the film.

senses.definition: The act of writing something again, especially to change or improve it.
examples: The rescripting of the show took several months.
Therapy sometimes involves the rescripting of painful memories.

senses.definition: The act of writing something again or differently.
examples: The document required complete rescription after the new law was passed.
Rescription of the contract terms delayed the agreement.

senses.definition: Relating to the act of writing something again or changing a written text.
examples: The committee took a rescriptive approach to the outdated policy.
Rescriptive changes were necessary to modernize the charter.

senses.definition: In a way that involves rewriting or revising a text.
examples: The rules were rescriptively updated to reflect new standards.
The document was handled rescriptively rather than being replaced.

senses.definition: Plural of rescript: official written answers or orders, especially from someone in authority.
examples: The archive contains several royal rescripts from that period.
Papal rescripts were often used to settle complex disputes.

senses.definition: To scrub something again to make it cleaner.
examples: You may need to rescrub the floor to remove the stains.
She had to rescrub the pan because it was still greasy.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of rescrub: scrubbed again.
examples: He rescrubbed the sink until it shone.
The workers rescrubbed the walls after noticing more dirt.

senses.definition: The act of scrubbing something again.
examples: Rescrubbing of the kitchen was necessary after the party.
The rescrubbing took longer than the original cleaning.

senses.definition: Plural of rescrutiny: new or repeated careful examinations.
examples: The reports faced several rescrutinies from the review board.
After the scandal, the company’s accounts underwent multiple rescrutinies.

senses.definition: To examine something carefully again.
examples: The committee decided to rescrutinize the data after new doubts appeared.
We must rescrutinize these results before publishing them.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of rescrutinize: examined carefully again.
examples: The figures were rescrutinized after the error was found.
Each application was rescrutinized by an independent expert.

senses.definition: Present participle of rescrutinize: examining something carefully again.
examples: They are rescrutinizing the contracts before signing.
Rescrutinizing the results revealed several issues.

senses.definition: A second or new careful examination of something.
examples: The figures were put under rescrutiny after complaints from investors.
Rescrutiny of the evidence changed the outcome of the case.

senses.definition: Able to be rescued or saved from danger or harm.
examples: Only a few of the trapped animals were still rescuable.
The workers were in a rescuable position thanks to their safety gear.

senses.definition: To save someone or something from a dangerous or harmful situation.
examples: Firefighters rescued the family from the burning house.
The dog was rescued from the river by a passer‑by.

senses.definition: The act of saving someone or something from danger.
examples: The helicopter arrived just in time for the rescue.
Their dramatic rescue was shown on the news.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of rescue: saved from danger or harm.
examples: The children were rescued from the collapsing building.
Several animals were rescued after the flood.

senses.definition: A person who is rescued from danger.
examples: Each rescuee was checked by medical staff on arrival.
The rescuee thanked the firefighters for their quick action.

senses.definition: Plural of rescuee: people who are rescued from danger.
examples: Food and water were given to all the rescuees.
The rescuees were reunited with their families at the shelter.

senses.definition: Unable to be rescued or without any hope of being saved.
examples: They felt trapped and completely rescueless in the storm.
The ship sank in a rescueless situation far from land.

senses.definition: A person who saves someone or something from danger.
examples: The rescuer pulled the child from the water.
Rescuers worked through the night after the earthquake.

senses.definition: Plural of rescuer: people who save someone or something from danger.
examples: The rescuers searched the collapsed building for survivors.
Many rescuers were praised for their bravery.

senses.definition: Third person singular of rescue: saves someone or something from danger.
examples: He often rescues abandoned animals.
The team rescues hikers who get lost in the mountains.

senses.definition: Plural of rescue: acts of saving people or things from danger.
examples: Several daring rescues were carried out during the storm.
The film shows real‑life rescues by the coast guard.

senses.definition: Present participle of rescue: saving someone or something from danger.
examples: They were busy rescuing people from the flooded houses.
Rescuing trapped animals can be very dangerous.

senses.definition: To shape or form something again, especially as an artwork or part of the body.
examples: The artist decided to resculpt the statue’s face.
Intense training helped resculpt his body.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of resculpt: shaped or formed again.
examples: The monument was resculpted after it was damaged.
Years of exercise had resculpted her figure.

senses.definition: The act of shaping or forming something again.
examples: The resculpting of the damaged statue took months.
The program focuses on the resculpting of the body through exercise.

senses.definition: Third person singular of resculpt: shapes or forms something again.
examples: Regular training resculpts the muscles over time.
The artist resculpts old pieces into new works.

senses.definition: A person who rescues someone or something.
examples: Each rescusser received an award for bravery.
The rescusser reached the victim just in time.

senses.definition: To seal or close something again so that nothing can get in or out.
examples: After taking some chips, remember to reseal the bag.
The plumber had to reseal the pipe to stop the leak.

senses.definition: To cover a surface again with a protective coating.
examples: You should reseal the wooden deck every few years.
The tiles need to be resealed to keep water out.

senses.definition: Designed so that it can be sealed again after opening.
examples: The nuts come in a resealable bag.
Store the leftovers in a resealable container.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of reseal: sealed again.
examples: She resealed the envelope after adding one more page.
The technician resealed the joint to prevent further leaks.

senses.definition: The act of sealing something again.
examples: Regular resealing of the driveway will protect it from water damage.
The resealing of the product keeps it fresh for longer.

senses.definition: Third person singular of reseal: seals or closes something again.
examples: This clip reseals the bag easily.
The machine automatically reseals the bottles after filling them.

senses.definition: To sew or join a seam again.
examples: The tailor had to reseam the trousers where the stitching had come undone.
You can reseam the edge to make it stronger.

senses.definition: Careful study of a subject to discover new facts or ideas.
examples: She is doing research on climate change.
Their research shows a link between diet and health.

senses.definition: To study a subject carefully to discover new information about it.
examples: He is researching ways to reduce waste.
We need to research the market before launching the product.

senses.definition: Possible to study or investigate in a serious, organized way.
examples: We need to turn this broad idea into a clear, researchable question.
Not all topics are easily researchable with the data we have.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of research: studied something carefully.
examples: She thoroughly researched the topic before writing the article.
We researched several options before making a decision.

senses.definition: Describing something that is based on careful study and investigation.
examples: It was a well‑researched report.
The book is highly researched and full of data.

senses.definition: A person who studies a subject carefully to discover new information.
examples: She works as a medical researcher at the university.
Researchers found a new way to treat the disease.

senses.definition: Plural of researcher: people who study a subject carefully to discover new information.
examples: Researchers around the world are working on a vaccine.
Many researchers disagree about the causes of the problem.

senses.definition: Third person singular of research: studies a subject carefully to discover new information.
examples: He researches consumer behavior for a marketing company.
She researches how technology affects education.

senses.definition: Plural of research: several studies on one or more subjects.
examples: His researches in physics changed the field.
The book summarizes many recent researches on the topic.

senses.definition: Full of careful study and investigation, or showing a strong habit of researching.
examples: It was a deeply researchful analysis of the problem.
Her approach is researchful, combining data from many sources.

senses.definition: Present participle of research: studying a subject carefully to discover new information.
examples: She is researching ways to make the product cheaper.
They are researching the history of the local area.
